 light / dark 

 map / reports / state of hermes — april 2026

 community report · q1 2026 
 The state of Hermes Agent — April 2026 
 A community report on the first six weeks of Nous Research's self-improving AI agent. 
 Published apr 11, 2026 · 10 min read · hermesatlas.com 

 TL;DR 

 Six weeks ago, Nous Research quietly released Hermes Agent — an open-source AI agent built around a single contrarian premise: what if your AI got smarter the more you used it, instead of resetting at the end of every session? The community noticed. The repo went from 0 to 57,200 GitHub stars in six weeks, growing faster than OpenClaw at the same stage. 

 But star counts only tell part of the story. The real signal is what's been built around it. In just six weeks, the community has shipped: 

 3 official Nous Research extensions (autonovel, hermes-paperclip-adapter, hermes-agent-self-evolution) 
 17 community skill libraries (including the 4,132-star Anthropic Cybersecurity Skills collection mapped to MITRE ATT&CK) 
 8 external memory providers (Honcho, Mem0, Hindsight, Supermemory, and others) 
 9 multi-agent orchestration frameworks (mission-control alone has 3,875 stars) 
 7 deployment templates spanning Docker, Nix, systemd, and managed cloud 
 80+ quality-filtered repos in total , growing at roughly 5,000 GitHub stars per week ecosystem-wide 

 This report unpacks how a model-research lab from upstate New York turned an open-source release into a movement, what Hermes does differently from OpenClaw and Claude Code, and what to watch for in Q2. 

 newsletter · free · 1–2× / month 

 subscribe 

 1. What Hermes Agent actually is 

 Hermes Agent is an autonomous AI agent that lives on a server (or your laptop, or a $5 VPS) and gets more capable over time. Three things make it different from every other AI agent framework released in the last two years: 

 1. A built-in learning loop. When Hermes completes a complex task — say, debugging a microservice or refactoring a Rust crate — it can synthesize that experience into a permanent skill document. These skills follow the agentskills.io open standard: structured Markdown with metadata, written in natural language, callable as slash commands. Next time the agent encounters something similar, it doesn't start from zero. It loads the relevant skill and gets to work. 

 2. Persistent multi-level memory. Hermes maintains a bounded MEMORY.md (the agent's notes) and USER.md (user profile) that always live in the system prompt. Below that, every session is stored in SQLite with FTS5 full-text search, so the agent can recall any past conversation. Above that, eight pluggable external memory providers (Honcho, Mem0, Hindsight, Supermemory, RetainDB, ByteRover, OpenViking, Holographic) handle long-term semantic memory and graph-based recall. This three-layer architecture is the defining technical decision of the project. 

 3. A unified gateway across 14 messaging platforms. A single Hermes instance can run as a Telegram bot, a Discord bot, a Slack app, a WhatsApp client, a Signal contact, an SMS endpoint, an Email auto-responder, a Matrix integration, a Mattermost bot, and more — all from the same daemon, with one shared session and one shared memory. You can start a conversation in CLI and continue it in Telegram, then ask it to send the result to your team in Slack. 

 The core repo is MIT-licensed Python (~9,200 lines in run_agent.py alone), supports 20+ LLM providers (OpenRouter, Nous Portal, Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, local Ollama, vLLM, etc.), and runs on six execution backends from local processes to Daytona cloud sandboxes. 

 2. The launch timeline 

 February 25, 2026: Nous Research drops Hermes Agent on GitHub with a simple tweet: "Meet Hermes Agent, the open source agent that grows with you." The launch post gets 557 likes — strong but not viral. 

 February 26-March 10: Tech press picks it up. MarkTechPost runs a piece framing it as a fix for "AI forgetfulness." Bitcoin News crosses the crypto/AI divide with an explainer. The Top AI Product blog calls it "the open-source AI agent that finally remembers everything." 

 March 11: Hits 22,000 GitHub stars and 242 contributors — this would have been a great six-month number for most projects. 

 March 15-25: The skill ecosystem explodes. Vercel Labs publishes their official vercel-labs/agent-skills library. Black Forest Labs (the FLUX image gen team) ships official Hermes-compatible image generation skills. Anthropic releases a 754-skill cybersecurity collection mapped to MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, and D3FEND. 

 March 23: Nous Research ships v0.4.0 — what Teknium calls "the platform expansion release." 200+ bug fixes, 6 new messaging adapters (Signal, DingTalk, SMS, Mattermost, Matrix, Webhook), MCP server management with OAuth 2.1, an OpenAI-compatible API server, and gateway prompt caching. The release is built from 300 PRs in five days. 

 April 3: Anthropic blocks OpenClaw from accessing Claude Code subscriptions. The Hacker News thread reaches #1 with 1,064 points and 811 comments. Nous Research immediately tweets: "If you're having trouble with your lobster-themed agent since the recent update, try downloading Hermes Agent, then running 'hermes claw migrate'. We've been told this helps a lot." The cheeky migration tool tweet picks up 813 likes. Migration traffic spikes. 

 April 8: v0.8.0 ships with 209 PRs and 82 issue closures. Headline features: live model switching mid-session, free Xiaomi MiMo v2 Pro via Nous Portal, native Google AI Studio (Gemini) integration, Matrix tier-1 support, MCP OAuth 2.1 with PKCE plus OSV malware scanning, and the long-awaited notify_on_complete for background tasks. Manim skill ships the same week and goes viral after Shopify CEO Tobi Lütke uses it to generate a 3Blue1Brown-style math animation in one prompt. 

 April 11: 57,200+ GitHub stars , 7,572 forks, 274 contributors. The ecosystem map at hermesatlas.com now tracks 80+ community projects. Nous Portal partnerships announced with MiniMax (M2.7) and Xiaomi (free MiMo v2 Pro for two weeks). Paradigm + a16z confirmed as Nous Research's lead investors. 

 That's six weeks. 

 3. Growth in numbers 

 Metric As of April 11, 2026 
 
 GitHub stars (core repo) 57,200 
 Forks 7,572 
 Contributors 274+ 
 Issues closed 800+ 
 Releases 8 (v0.1 &rarr; v0.8) 
 Messaging platforms 14 
 Community ecosystem repos 80+ 
 External memory providers 8 
 Total ecosystem stars 90,750 

 Growth velocity: Roughly 9,500 stars per week on the core repo over the last 30 days. For context, OpenClaw (which had a multi-month head start) is currently growing at ~3,000 stars per week. Hermes Agent has flipped OpenClaw on weekly star growth. 

 Contributor profile: Teknium (Nous Research co-founder) leads the commit count with 179 PRs in v0.8 alone. Top community contributors include @SHL0MS , @alt-glitch , @benbarclay , @CharlieKerfoot , and @WAXLYY . Fourteen distinct contributors merged PRs in the v0.8 release window — a healthy bus factor for a six-week-old project. 

 International reach: Coverage has expanded well beyond English-language tech press. Chinese AI developer @wangray published a deep technical analysis of Hermes's 4-layer memory system that picked up 258 likes and 49,000 views. Japanese developers have shipped install tutorials. The Chinese AI community has noted Nous Research's Web3 connections (Paradigm + a16z) as a sign of credible long-term backing. 

 4. The three-way positioning: Hermes vs OpenClaw vs Claude Code 

 A fair comparison matters here, because the community keeps trying to frame this as a winner-takes-all competition, and that's not really what's happening. 

 Dimension Claude Code OpenClaw Hermes Agent 
 
 Core identity Pair-programmer in your IDE Operations platform for teams Personal assistant that learns 
 Architecture IDE-integrated Gateway control plane (Node.js) Runtime AIAgent loop (Python) 
 Memory model Auto-notes to disk Unbounded Markdown Bounded curation + SQLite FTS5 + 8 provider plugins 
 Skills Static, human-authored Static (5,700+ on ClawHub) Autonomously created + refined by the agent 
 Channels IDE only 22+ including iMessage, IRC, LINE 14 including Matrix, DingTalk, Feishu 
 License Proprietary MIT MIT 
 Stars N/A (Anthropic product) ~250,000 57,200 
 Execution Local sandbox Local Local + Docker + SSH + Modal + Daytona + Singularity 

 Where each one wins: 

 Claude Code wins for focused coding sessions in an IDE. It's the best in-editor pair programmer Anthropic could build, and it integrates deeply with VS Code, Zed, and JetBrains. If you want an AI that helps you write code in your editor, this is the answer. 
 OpenClaw wins for team operations and channel breadth. With 22 channel integrations and a 5,700-skill marketplace, it's the most mature option for running a single agent across an entire company's communication surface. The gateway-first architecture is a strength for ops workflows. 
 Hermes Agent wins for the learning loop and long-horizon work. If you want an agent that gets better at your specific workflows over time — that remembers your project's quirks, that builds reusable skills automatically, that you can trust to run cron jobs unsupervised — Hermes is currently the only credible option. 

 The community consensus, repeated across Reddit, X, Substack, and the Hermes Agent Discord: they're complementary, not competitive. A typical setup uses Claude Code for IDE coding, Hermes Agent for personal automation and learning, and OpenClaw or Slack-native bots for team-wide ops. 

 5. The architectural bet that defines Hermes 

 If you read just one section of this report, read this one. Hermes Agent's design embodies a specific bet about where AI agents are going, and that bet differs sharply from its competition. 

 The bet: Memory-bounded, deliberately curated agents will outperform unbounded "remember everything" agents in real-world use, because the constraint forces consolidation and consolidation produces better priors. 

 OpenClaw's memory is unbounded. You can dump anything into it. The agent reads everything every turn. The bet is that more context = better answers. 

 Hermes's memory is strictly bounded : 2,200 characters for MEMORY.md , 1,375 for USER.md . When you hit the limit, you can't just keep adding — you have to consolidate. The agent must explicitly decide what's worth remembering. This is a constraint that forces the agent to develop a working theory of what matters about you and your work, and then refine that theory as it gets new evidence. 

 It's the same bet that LLMs make about attention: you can't attend to everything, so you have to learn what to attend to. Hermes applies that bet at the memory layer. 

 The early evidence suggests it's working. Users consistently report that Hermes "feels different" after 2-3 weeks of use in a way that other agents don't. One r/LocalLLaMA user reported a 40% speedup on repeated research tasks after Hermes auto-generated three skill documents over two hours. Another user — quoted on X by Robert Scoble — said "my Hermes agent spooked me with a very specific detail from a setup I was discussing with it 3 weeks ago." 

 Whether this bet is correct is still an open question. But it's a real bet, and it's the first credible alternative to the "infinite context window" school of thought that has dominated AI agent design for the past two years. 

 6. Community sentiment 

 Tracking community sentiment across X, Reddit, Substack, Hacker News, and the Hermes Agent Discord (~4,000 builders), three themes dominate: 

 Positive: 
 
 "The first agent harness that you immediately know was developed by people who have a vast knowledge" — @DataDeLaurier 
 "Much less token hungry" than competitors — repeated across r/LocalLLaMA 
 "Excellent right out of the box" with a "5-minute install" — repeated by multiple users 
 " @NousResearch may have cooked with hermes agent" — @fujikanaeda (retweeted by Nous itself) 
 "Hermes is definitely a better coder, id drop the claw altogether lol" — @teknium 

 Critical (acknowledged honestly): 
 
 Some users report edge case issues with extended use; one well-known user ( @ksubedi ) returned to OpenClaw after testing 
 Local model performance can be slower than running the model directly through LMStudio (one user reported 1-2 tokens/s through Hermes vs 45 tokens/s native) 
 Setup, while generally praised, was called "more tedious than OpenClaw" by @chrysb in the most-liked critical post 

 The signal-to-noise ratio strongly favors Hermes. Out of dozens of posts I've reviewed, the negative ones are specific and technical (and generally being addressed in releases), while the positive ones tend to be more emotional and broader ("magical," "spooked me," "the future"). 

 7. The ecosystem map 

 Here's the breakdown of what the community has built around Hermes Agent in six weeks. Numbers reflect star counts as of April 11, 2026. 

 Category Count Top project (stars) 
 
 Core & Official 6 hermes-agent (57.2K) 
 Skills & Registries 17 Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills (4.1K) 
 Memory & Context 6 hindsight (8.4K) 
 Multi-Agent & Orchestration 7 mission-control (3.9K) 
 Workspaces & GUIs 4 hermes-workspace (830) 
 Plugins & Extensions 6 hermes-web-search-plus (21) 
 Deployment & Infra 7 llm-agents.nix (967) 
 Integrations & Bridges 5 hermes-android (38) 
 Developer Tools 9 tokscale (1.7K) 
 Domain Applications 8 DashClaw (204) 
 Guides & Docs 5 awesome-hermes-agent (898) 
 Forks & Derivatives 3 hermes-agent-camel (12) 

 The full quality-filtered, security-reviewed, live-data-updated map is available at hermesatlas.com . 

 The most surprising entries — to me, at least — are the cross-pollinations. Hermes-android puts Hermes on your phone via a Python bridge. Hermes-blockchain-oracle is a Solana intelligence MCP server. Hermes-embodied uses Hermes to fine-tune VLA robotics models on cloud GPUs. Hermes-mars-rover simulates Mars exploration with ROS2 and Gazebo. The fact that the ecosystem is already this diverse six weeks in suggests Hermes hit a real nerve. 

 8. What to watch for in Q2 

 Five things I'd watch closely over the next 90 days: 

 1. The skill marketplace network effect. Hermes ships 40+ bundled skills today. The skills hub (built into the CLI) browses agentskills.io, GitHub taps, skills.sh, and ClawHub. If the rate of community skill creation continues to accelerate, Hermes could have a more curated (smaller, but higher-quality) skill marketplace than OpenClaw's by mid-Q2. The wildcard: whether community skills meaningfully cross over from OpenClaw via the OGP (Open Gateway Protocol) federation that both projects support. 

 2. The multi-instance enterprise story. Hermes profiles let you run isolated agents per client/project on the same machine. If a few high-profile companies start using this for internal automation (DM Slack bot for engineering, separate Telegram bot for sales, all sharing infrastructure), Hermes becomes a real competitor to enterprise agent platforms. Watch for Hermes to ship per-tenant memory isolation and audit logging in v0.9 or v0.10. 

 3. The self-improvement narrative becoming measurable. Right now, "the agent that grows with you" is a tagline backed by anecdotal evidence. If Nous Research publishes benchmark numbers showing measurable improvement on repeated task families over time — real numbers, not vibes — Hermes becomes impossible to ignore. 

 4. The Nous Portal subscription economics. Nous gives away free tier access with promotional codes (AGENTHERMES01) and free MiMo v2 Pro for two weeks. At some point this has to convert to a sustainable subscription business. 

 5. The "first deletion" moment. Every fast-growing open-source project eventually faces a moment where its community has to decide what to remove from the core. Hermes is approaching this: 47 built-in tools, 14 platforms, 6 backends, 8 memory providers. At some point, things will need to move out of core into optional plugins. How Nous handles this transition — and whether the community trusts them with it — will define the next phase. 

 Methodology: Synthesized from 27 research files including official documentation, GitHub release notes, press coverage, X/Twitter analysis, Reddit discussion, and the awesome-hermes-agent curated list. All 80+ ecosystem repos are independently security-reviewed and live-tracked. 
 Not affiliated with Nous Research. Hermes Atlas is an independent community project. 
 Corrections welcome: open an issue 
 if this was useful, drop a ★ on the atlas repo → 
 explore the ecosystem map →

 stay in the loop 
 new research, reports, and project drops from across the Hermes ecosystem — 1–2× per month. no spam.

 v2 · 2026.04

# Project Catalog (100 projects)

---

## NousResearch/hermes-agent

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/hermes-agent
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 83,290
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
The self-improving AI agent that grows with you — persistent memory, auto-generated skills, 14 platforms, 6 execution backends

Hermes Agent is an autonomous AI system designed for persistent, cross-platform task execution and self-improvement. It utilizes a closed learning loop to autonomously generate new skills from experience and build long-term user models through periodic memory nudges and session searches. The agent operates across 14 messaging platforms and supports six execution backends, including Docker and SSH, for secure tool use. Users can switch between various LLM providers and automate recurring tasks via a built-in natural language cron scheduler.

Highlights:
- Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement from task experience
- Supports 14 messaging platforms including Telegram, Discord, and Slack
- Six execution backends for local, containerized, or serverless persistence

---

## garrytan/gbrain

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/garrytan/gbrain
GitHub: https://github.com/garrytan/gbrain
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 8,857
Garry's Opinionated OpenClaw/Hermes Agent Brain

GBrain is an opinionated memory and context layer designed to give AI agents a persistent, self-wiring knowledge base. It utilizes a hybrid search system and a self-constructing knowledge graph to extract entities and create typed links between people, companies, and events without requiring LLM calls for graph construction. The system consolidates memory through overnight autonomous jobs that fix citations and enrich data from meetings, emails, and media. It integrates with Hermes Agent and OpenClaw, offering 29 specialized skills for automated workflow execution and information retrieval.

Highlights:
- Hybrid search combining vector retrieval with a self-wiring knowledge graph.
- Automated entity extraction and typed relationship linking for structured memory.
- Exposes 30+ MCP tools for integration with Claude Code and Cursor.

---

## vectorize-io/hindsight

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/vectorize-io/hindsight
GitHub: https://github.com/vectorize-io/hindsight
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 8,362
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Agent memory that learns — long-term retain/recall/reflect workflows

Hindsight is an agent memory system designed to help AI agents learn and evolve through long-term retention, recall, and reflection workflows. It utilizes biomimetic data structures to organize information similarly to human memory, moving beyond standard vector search or knowledge graphs. The system provides an LLM wrapper for automatic memory management and supports manual control via Python and Node.js SDKs. It is optimized for conversational AI and autonomous agents that require per-user personalization and behavioral adaptation based on feedback.

Highlights:
- Implements retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term agent learning
- Uses biomimetic data structures to organize facts and mental models
- Integrates with major LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama

---

## mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills
GitHub: https://github.com/mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 4,132
754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, ATLAS, D3FEND & NIST AI RMF

Anthropic Cybersecurity Skills is a library of 754 structured security skills designed to provide AI agents with the domain-specific workflows of a senior analyst. It utilizes the agentskills.io open standard, providing YAML-frontmatter for discovery and structured Markdown for step-by-step execution across 26 security domains. Each skill is uniquely mapped to five industry frameworks, including MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, and the NIST AI RMF. The project is compatible with major AI platforms such as Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, and Hermes Agent to assist in threat hunting and incident response.

Highlights:
- 754 structured skills mapped to MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0
- Covers 26 security domains including Cloud, Malware Analysis, and ICS
- Compatible with agentskills.io standard for seamless AI agent integration

---

## builderz-labs/mission-control

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/builderz-labs/mission-control
GitHub: https://github.com/builderz-labs/mission-control
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 3,875
Self-hosted AI agent orchestration — dispatch tasks, run multi-agent workflows, monitor spend

Mission Control is a self-hosted dashboard designed to orchestrate and monitor fleets of AI agents. It utilizes a local-first architecture powered by SQLite and Next.js to coordinate multi-agent workflows, dispatch tasks, and track operational costs without external database dependencies. The platform features built-in quality gates for task approval, real-time telemetry via WebSockets, and a security framework that includes secret detection and trust scoring. It integrates with various agent frameworks like CrewAI and LangGraph, and provides a bridge for CLI tools such as Claude Code.

Highlights:
- Self-hosted orchestration using SQLite with zero external dependencies
- Multi-gateway support for CrewAI, LangGraph, AutoGen, and Claude SDK
- Built-in Aegis review system for human-in-the-loop task sign-off

---

## nesquena/hermes-webui

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/nesquena/hermes-webui
GitHub: https://github.com/nesquena/hermes-webui
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 2,479
Hermes WebUI: The best way to use Hermes Agent from the web or from your phone!

Hermes WebUI provides a lightweight, browser-based interface for the Hermes Agent, offering a visual alternative to the standard command-line environment. Built with Python and vanilla JavaScript, the application maintains full parity with the CLI without requiring complex build steps or frameworks. The interface features a three-panel layout for managing chat sessions, workspace files, and agent controls like model selection and token usage monitoring. It allows users to interact with their agent's persistent memory, self-improving skills, and scheduled jobs through a secure web or mobile-friendly portal.

Highlights:
- Full feature parity with the Hermes Agent CLI experience
- Three-panel layout for chat, file browsing, and session management
- Zero-build architecture using Python and vanilla JavaScript

---

## EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui
GitHub: https://github.com/EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 1,844
Web dashboard for Hermes Agent — multi-platform AI chat, session management, scheduled jobs, usage analytics & channel configuration (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp)

Hermes Web UI is a comprehensive dashboard for managing Hermes Agent, providing a centralized interface for AI interactions and system administration. It simplifies agent operations by integrating multi-session chat, usage analytics, and automated cron job scheduling into a single responsive web environment. Users can configure eight different communication platforms, manage model providers, and browse files across local or remote backends. The system also features multi-profile management, group chat rooms with @mention routing, and an integrated web terminal for direct command-line access.

Highlights:
- Unified configuration for Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, and four other platforms
- Real-time usage analytics tracking token consumption, costs, and model distribution
- Multi-backend file browser supporting local, Docker, SSH, and Singularity environments

## junhoyeo/tokscale

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/junhoyeo/tokscale
GitHub: https://github.com/junhoyeo/tokscale
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 1,690
CLI tool for tracking token usage from Claude Code, OpenClaw, Hermes, Codex, Gemini, and more

Tokscale is a high-performance CLI tool and visualization dashboard designed to track token consumption and costs across various AI coding agents. It works by scanning local session databases and logs from supported clients to aggregate usage data into a native Rust TUI or a web-based 3D contribution graph. The tool integrates with LiteLLM pricing data to provide real-time cost calculations, including tiered pricing and cache discounts. It supports a wide range of agents including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Cursor.

Highlights:
- Tracks token usage across 20+ AI coding agents and CLIs
- Provides real-time cost calculations using LiteLLM pricing data
- Features a native Rust TUI and 3D visualization dashboard

---

## NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 1,249
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Function calling examples and training data for Hermes LLM models

This repository provides the official implementation and examples for function calling and JSON mode using the Hermes 2 Pro model. It utilizes a recursive loop to generate, execute, and process tool calls based on structured schemas, specifically supporting financial data retrieval via yfinance. Users can define custom tools using Python decorators or enforce structured outputs by serializing Pydantic models into JSON schemas. The project demonstrates multi-turn interactions using the ChatML format to maintain state between tool responses and assistant replies.

Highlights:
- Enables recursive function calling and structured JSON mode inference
- Supports custom tool definition via Python decorators and Pydantic
- Uses ChatML prompt format for OpenAI-compatible multi-turn dialogue

---

## NousResearch/atropos

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/atropos
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/atropos
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 997
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
RL training environments framework for tool-calling models

Atropos is an environment microservice framework designed for asynchronous reinforcement learning with large language models. It operates by setting up environments as services that communicate via a trajectory API, allowing trainers to pull data batches while models interact with external tools. The system supports diverse training scenarios including code execution, multi-turn tool calling, and multimodal tasks. It has been used to significantly improve model performance on benchmarks like the Berkeley Function Calling Benchmark.

Highlights:
- Microservice architecture for scalable asynchronous reinforcement learning environments
- Standardized trajectory API for collecting and distributing model interaction data
- Supports code execution, game-based learning, and RLAIF reward models

---

## numtide/llm-agents.nix

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/numtide/llm-agents.nix
GitHub: https://github.com/numtide/llm-agents.nix
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 967
Nix packages for AI coding agents including Hermes

llm-agents.nix provides a collection of Nix packages designed to streamline the installation and management of AI coding agents and development tools. It offers a centralized repository of packages that are automatically updated on a daily basis. Users can easily deploy a variety of terminal-based agents, orchestrators, and proxy APIs for diverse AI providers. This ecosystem supports diverse workflows, ranging from local agent execution to multi-agent coordination.

Highlights:
- Daily automatic updates for all included packages
- Supports diverse AI agents like Claude Code and Goose
- Includes multi-agent orchestrators and unified proxy APIs

Curated list of awesome skills, tools, integrations, and resources for Hermes Agent

This repository is a curated collection of resources, tools, and community-developed skills designed for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It organizes the landscape of the self-improving AI agent by categorizing official Nous Research projects, third-party skill libraries, and deployment utilities. Users can find specialized integrations for messaging platforms like Telegram and Discord, as well as meta-skills that allow the agent to auto-generate its own capabilities from workflows. The list serves as a central hub for developers to extend the agent's memory, reasoning, and task-execution layers.

Highlights:
- Tracks official Nous Research core repositories and documentation
- Catalogs community skills for goal management and literate programming
- Lists multi-platform bridges for Telegram, Discord, and Slack

## outsourc-e/hermes-workspace

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/outsourc-e/hermes-workspace
GitHub: https://github.com/outsourc-e/hermes-workspace
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 830
Native web workspace — chat, terminal, memory browser, skills manager, and inspector panel

Hermes Workspace is a native web-based command center designed to provide a unified graphical interface for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It integrates directly with the Hermes gateway via real-time SSE streaming to manage chat, terminal access, and tool call rendering. Users can browse and edit agent memory, manage over 2,000 skills, and orchestrate complex jobs through a security-hardened dashboard. The project follows a zero-fork architecture, allowing it to run on vanilla installations of the Nous Research hermes-agent without custom patches.

Highlights:
- Real-time SSE streaming for chat and tool call rendering
- Comprehensive memory browser and skill manager for agent orchestration
- Security-hardened interface with execution approval prompts and auth middleware

---

## NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 778
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Evolutionary self-improvement using DSPy + GEPA — optimizes skills, prompts, and code

Hermes Agent Self-Evolution is an automated optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes Agent without requiring GPU training. The system utilizes DSPy and Genetic-Pareto Prompt Evolution (GEPA) to analyze execution traces and mutate skill files, prompts, and tool descriptions. It validates candidate improvements through a rigorous pipeline of synthetic or session-based evaluations, constraint gates, and mandatory test suites. The final output is a refined version of the agent's components delivered via pull requests for human review.

Highlights:
- Optimizes skills and prompts using reflective evolutionary search via API calls.
- Analyzes execution traces to understand and fix specific failure points.
- Enforces strict guardrails including size limits and 100% test pass rates.

---

## conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing
GitHub: https://github.com/conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 759
Audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI writing patterns

avoid-ai-writing is a portable skill for AI agents designed to identify and eliminate detectable AI writing patterns. It utilizes a structured audit process featuring 36 pattern categories and a three-tier word replacement table to replace AI-isms with plainer language. The tool operates in either a rewrite mode with a built-in second-pass audit or a detection-only mode for content scanning. It is compatible with agentskills.io-compliant agents, including Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes.

Highlights:
- Two-pass audit to catch surviving AI patterns after rewriting
- 109-entry word replacement table across three severity tiers
- Compatible with Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes Agent

---

## greyhaven-ai/autocontext

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/greyhaven-ai/autocontext
GitHub: https://github.com/greyhaven-ai/autocontext
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 711
Recursive self-improving context harness — helps agents succeed on complex tasks

autocontext is a recursive self-improving harness designed to help LLM agents succeed on complex tasks by turning repeated work into validated, reusable knowledge. It operates through a structured multi-agent loop where competitors propose strategies, analysts evaluate outcomes, and coaches update playbooks to ensure agents improve across generations. The system supports various execution surfaces including simulations, evidence-driven investigations, and verifier-driven missions. Users can eventually distill stable agent behaviors into cheaper local runtimes using exported training data.

Highlights:
- Accumulates validated knowledge to improve agent performance across repeated runs
- Multi-agent loop featuring competitor, analyst, coach, architect, and curator roles
- Supports browser-integrated investigations and verifier-driven mission checkpoints

---

## NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 663
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Run Hermes as a managed employee in Paperclip company systems

The hermes-paperclip-adapter is a bridge that integrates Hermes Agent into the Paperclip company management system as a managed employee. It functions by wrapping the Hermes CLI, parsing raw terminal output into structured transcript entries and GFM markdown for clean rendering in the Paperclip UI. The adapter enables agents to respond to task assignments or issue comments while maintaining persistent memory and session continuity across heartbeats. It also synchronizes local and managed skills, allowing Hermes to utilize its full suite of 30+ native tools and MCP servers within a corporate organizational structure.

Highlights:
- Connects Hermes Agent to Paperclip as a managed autonomous employee
- Parses raw CLI output into structured tool cards and markdown
- Supports 8 inference providers including Anthropic, OpenAI, and OpenRouter

---

## wondelai/skills

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/wondelai/skills
GitHub: https://github.com/wondelai/skills
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 480
Cross-platform skills library for Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents

Wondel.ai Skills is a cross-platform library of agent capabilities designed for Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible environments. It provides pre-configured skill collections based on established industry frameworks, such as Jobs to Be Done, Clean Code, and Lean Startup methodology. Users can install these skills via the Claude Code marketplace or a dedicated CLI to equip AI agents with specialized knowledge in software architecture, UX design, and product strategy. The project bridges the gap between raw LLM capabilities and structured professional methodologies through modular, installable plugins.

Highlights:
- Compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents
- Modular installation via marketplace or npx skills CLI
- Frameworks for architecture, UX design, marketing, and product strategy

## NousResearch/autonovel

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/autonovel
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/autonovel
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 401
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Autonomous novel-writing pipeline generating 100k+ word manuscripts

Autonovel is an autonomous agent pipeline designed to transform a seed concept into a complete, publishable novel. It utilizes a multi-phase workflow that includes world-building, sequential drafting, and an adversarial revision loop involving dual-persona LLM reviewers. The system integrates mechanical slop detection and voice fingerprinting to ensure prose quality and narrative consistency across tens of thousands of words. Beyond text generation, the project automates typesetting in LaTeX, cover art creation via fal.ai, and multi-voice audiobook production using ElevenLabs.

Highlights:
- Autonomous four-phase pipeline from world-building to final export
- Adversarial revision loop with mechanical and LLM-based evaluation
- Automated typesetting, cover illustration, and multi-voice audiobook generation

---

## xaspx/hermes-control-interface

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/xaspx/hermes-control-interface
GitHub: https://github.com/xaspx/hermes-control-interface
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 323
A self-hosted web dashboard for the Hermes AI agent stack. Provides a browser-based terminal, file explorer, session overview, cron management, system metrics, and an agent status panel — all behind a

The Hermes Control Interface is a self-hosted web dashboard designed to manage the Hermes AI agent stack through a centralized graphical interface. It utilizes a Node.js and Express backend with WebSockets to provide real-time terminal access, file management, and multi-agent gateway control. Users can monitor system metrics, track token usage analytics, and configure agent skills or cron jobs without using the command line. The platform includes a revamped chat interface with tool call visualization and a robust role-based access control system for team management.

Highlights:
- Multi-agent gateway for starting, stopping, and configuring multiple profiles.
- Real-time chat interface with session management and tool call cards.
- Detailed token analytics tracking costs by model, platform, and time.

## alchaincyf/hermes-agent-orange-book

GitHub: https://github.com/alchaincyf/hermes-agent-orange-book
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 315
Hermes Agent practical guide — Orange Book series (Chinese language)

The Hermes Agent Orange Book is a practical guide designed to help users master the Hermes Agent open-source AI framework. It explains the framework's technical architecture, including its three-layer memory system, self-improving learning loop, and automatic skill evolution. The guide provides 17 chapters of content covering installation, customization, and real-world applications such as development automation and knowledge assistants.

Highlights:
- Detailed guides on memory systems and learning loops
- Step-by-step setup and multi-platform customization instructions
- Practical scenarios for dev automation and multi-agent systems

## dodo-reach/hermes-desktop

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/dodo-reach/hermes-desktop
GitHub: https://github.com/dodo-reach/hermes-desktop
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 313
A native Mac workspace for Hermes: real SSH, real terminal, real session data.

Hermes Desktop is a native macOS application that provides a localized workspace for managing Hermes Agent hosts over SSH. It connects directly to remote environments to manage sessions, skills, and cron jobs without requiring a gateway API, local file mirroring, or remote daemon. The app features a multi-tabbed terminal and profile-aware views that surface usage trends and canonical file states directly from the host. This allows users to maintain a persistent, native interface for multi-agent workflows while keeping the remote server as the single source of truth.

Highlights:
- Direct SSH connection with no remote helper service required
- Profile-aware management of sessions, skills, and cron jobs
- Native multi-tabbed terminal with live theme and color controls

---

## swarmclawai/swarmclaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/swarmclawai/swarmclaw
GitHub: https://github.com/swarmclawai/swarmclaw
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 285
Build autonomous AI agent swarms with orchestration, skills, and multiple model providers

SwarmClaw is a self-hosted runtime designed for orchestrating and managing autonomous AI agent swarms. It operates by providing a centralized environment for agent delegation, memory management, and runtime skill acquisition through an MCP-native architecture. The platform supports over 20 model providers and CLI backends, including Hermes, OpenClaw, and various local or cloud-based LLMs. Users can visualize agent hierarchies via an organizational chart and automate workflows using heartbeats and scheduled tasks.

Highlights:
- Supports 23 model providers including Hermes and OpenClaw
- MCP-native architecture with runtime skill learning and memory
- Visualizes agent teams and delegation via interactive org charts

---

## ksimback/hermes-ecosystem

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/ksimback/hermes-ecosystem
GitHub: https://github.com/ksimback/hermes-ecosystem
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 263
Hermes Atlas — the community map of every tool, skill, and integration for Hermes Agent by Nous Research

Hermes Atlas is a community-curated directory and ecosystem map for the Hermes Agent by Nous Research. It aggregates over 80 quality-filtered repositories, including skills, plugins, and deployment templates, using a vanilla JavaScript frontend and Vercel serverless functions. The platform features a RAG-powered chatbot that answers technical questions grounded in 27 research files using hybrid retrieval and OpenRouter LLMs. Users can search and filter the ecosystem while viewing live GitHub star counts and growth trends cached via Redis.

Highlights:
- Directory of 80+ security-reviewed Hermes Agent tools and integrations
- RAG chatbot providing grounded answers from 27 research files
- Live GitHub star tracking with Redis-cached sparklines and trends

---

## clawshell/clawshell

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/clawshell/clawshell
GitHub: https://github.com/clawshell/clawshell
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 255
The Runtime Security Layer for OpenClaw/Hermes-agent, the essential safety harness for PII & sensitive credentials protection.

ClawShell is a security-privileged process that acts as a safety harness for the Hermes Agent ecosystem by protecting sensitive credentials and PII. It functions as a proxy between the agent and upstream LLM providers, swapping virtual keys for real ones stored in a restricted directory to ensure the agent never has direct access to credentials. The system performs Data Loss Prevention (DLP) scanning to redact or block sensitive information in request and response bodies. Additionally, it provides secure email isolation through IMAP sender filtering and supports OAuth-based authentication for providers like OpenAI.

Highlights:
- Maps virtual keys to real credentials stored in protected Unix directories
- Scans and redacts PII in request and response bodies via regex
- Enforces sender-based email filtering for secure mailbox access

---

## DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills
GitHub: https://github.com/DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 220
Type-safe agentskills.io support with progressive disclosure for Pydantic AI

pydantic-ai-skills is a framework that implements the Agent Skills open specification to provide modular, specialized capabilities for Pydantic AI agents. It uses a tool-calling approach to enable progressive disclosure, allowing agents to discover and load detailed instructions or scripts only when relevant to a task. The package supports skills defined as filesystem directories or created programmatically using Python decorators and dataclasses. This architecture reduces token consumption by preventing the injection of unnecessary context into the initial system prompt.

Highlights:
- Implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI
- Reduces token usage through progressive disclosure of skill metadata
- Supports both filesystem-based and programmatic Python skill definitions

---

## ucsandman/DashClaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/ucsandman/DashClaw
GitHub: https://github.com/ucsandman/DashClaw
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 204
Decision infrastructure with guard policies and risk assessment for agents

DashClaw is a decision governance infrastructure designed to intercept and evaluate agent actions before they execute. It functions as a middleware layer between AI agents and external systems, enforcing guard policies and conducting risk assessments to prevent destructive actions. The platform provides verifiable evidence of every decision and supports human-in-the-loop approvals via Discord. It integrates with major frameworks like LangChain, CrewAI, and Claude Code through MCP servers, SDKs, or zero-code hooks.

Highlights:
- Intercepts agent tool calls to enforce safety and guard policies
- Provides real-time risk assessment and Discord-based manual approval workflows
- Records verifiable evidence and audit trails for every agent decision

---

## clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp
GitHub: https://github.com/clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 194
React SPA dashboard for Telegram Mini App v2.0 — 10-page mobile-first UI. Runs locally.

Hermes Telegram Mini App is a React-based terminal interface that allows users to manage and interact with Hermes agents directly within the Telegram ecosystem. It establishes a secure mobile-first workspace by wrapping the Hermes CLI in a dark-mode web UI, featuring streaming chat and system monitoring. The application utilizes dual HMAC-SHA256 and Ed25519 validation to ensure secure access via Telegram's authentication layer. Users can spawn background agent instances, manage scheduled cron jobs, and perform local OCR or vision analysis on uploaded files.

Highlights:
- Terminal-style chat with streaming responses and slash command support
- Background agent spawning with live output monitoring and interactive modes
- Integrated system health gauges for CPU, memory, and disk usage

---

## stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm
GitHub: https://github.com/stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 178
Lossless Context Management plugin for Hermes Agent — DAG-based context engine that never loses a message

Hermes-LCM is a Lossless Context Management plugin for Hermes Agent that prevents information loss during long-running conversations. It replaces standard lossy compression with a hierarchical Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) that persists every message in an immutable-first SQLite store. When context limits are reached, the engine uses three levels of escalation—from detailed summaries to deterministic truncation—while providing the agent with specialized tools to retrieve original details. This architecture allows agents to maintain a bounded active context window without losing the ability to drill back into compacted history through structured retrieval.

Highlights:
- Hierarchical DAG summaries with three-level escalation for context compaction
- Immutable-first SQLite storage ensures no message data is ever deleted
- Agent tools for grep, description, and expansion of compacted history

---

## Web3CZ/Web3Hermes

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Web3CZ/Web3Hermes
GitHub: https://github.com/Web3CZ/Web3Hermes
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 140
【Hermes WebUI】Hermes-Agent 中文版 WebUI，便于中国用户使用

Web3Hermes is a lightweight WebUI for Hermes Agent specifically localized for Chinese-speaking users. It provides a desktop-optimized interface that simplifies interaction with the agent through a localized workflow and full Chinese language support. The platform integrates a built-in workspace file browser, session management tools, and support for multiple AI models including OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepSeek. Users can manage tasks, edit code with syntax highlighting, and visualize tool calls within a responsive dark or light themed environment.

Highlights:
- Localized Chinese interface with dark and light theme support
- Integrated workspace file browser for direct file editing and management
- Comprehensive session management including export, search, and tool call visualization

---

## Agents365-ai/drawio-skill

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Agents365-ai/drawio-skill
GitHub: https://github.com/Agents365-ai/drawio-skill
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 131
Generate draw.io diagrams from natural language descriptions

The drawio-skill project is an agentic tool that converts natural language descriptions into professional .drawio diagrams. It utilizes a set of six diagram presets and a grid-aligned layout engine to generate XML files that can be exported to PNG, SVG, or PDF via the draw.io desktop CLI. The system features a self-correcting iterative loop that reads exported files to auto-fix layout issues and supports specialized ML model diagrams with tensor shape annotations. It integrates across multiple AI platforms including Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes Agent.

Highlights:
- Generates six diagram types including UML, ERD, and ML architectures
- Supports animated connectors and 10px grid-aligned coordinate snapping
- Automates iterative refinement with a 5-round self-check safety valve

---

## elkimek/honcho-self-hosted

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/elkimek/honcho-self-hosted
GitHub: https://github.com/elkimek/honcho-self-hosted
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 126
Self-hosted Honcho memory backend for cross-session persistence

This project provides a configuration layer for self-hosting Plastic Labs' Honcho memory backend, enabling cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent without relying on third-party cloud storage. It deploys the full Honcho stack—including the API, PostgreSQL with pgvector, and Redis—on local hardware using Docker Compose. Users can route memory extraction and reasoning tasks through any OpenAI-compatible API or local inference servers like Ollama to maintain data sovereignty. This setup allows Hermes to build long-term user profiles and logical observations while keeping conversation data on the user's own infrastructure.

Highlights:
- Enables private cross-session memory for Hermes Agent via self-hosting
- Supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM provider or local inference servers
- Includes automated setup script for Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis

---

## AaronWong1999/hermesclaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/AaronWong1999/hermesclaw
GitHub: https://github.com/AaronWong1999/hermesclaw
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 112
Run Hermes Agent and OpenClaw on the same WeChat account

HermesClaw is an integration bridge that enables a single WeChat account to run both Hermes Agent and OpenClaw simultaneously. It functions as a central iLink poller that creates two local proxy servers, allowing each agent gateway to receive messages without token conflicts or 403 errors. Users can toggle between agents or trigger simultaneous responses using commands like /hermes, /openclaw, or /both. The tool maintains native support for voice transcriptions and media files by forwarding raw iLink protocol messages to the respective gateways.

Highlights:
- Enables dual-agent operation on a single WeChat iLink account
- Routes messages via /hermes, /openclaw, and /both commands
- Automated installation script patches existing gateway configurations

---

## yoloshii/ClawMem

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/yoloshii/ClawMem
GitHub: https://github.com/yoloshii/ClawMem
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 86
On-device memory and context engine for agents — local-first, no cloud dependencies

ClawMem is a local-first memory and context engine designed to provide persistent knowledge across different AI agent runtimes. It utilizes a hybrid retrieval architecture that combines vector search, BM25, and multi-graph traversal to surface relevant project documentation and past decisions. The system automatically captures session insights using local observer models and maintains a unified SQLite vault shared between Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes agents. By operating entirely on-device without cloud dependencies, it ensures data privacy while managing document lifecycles and causal relationships between facts.

Highlights:
- Unified local SQLite vault for cross-agent memory persistence
- Hybrid retrieval combining vector search, BM25, and graph traversal
- Automated decision capture and handoff generation via local models

---

## smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills
GitHub: https://github.com/smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 85
Oracle network and smart contract interaction skills (agentskills.io spec)

Chainlink Agent Skills is a collection of standardized tools that enable AI agents to interact with Chainlink oracle services and smart contracts. The project implements the Agent Skills specification, allowing agents to discover and execute specific blockchain functions through a modular CLI-based installation. These skills provide capabilities for cross-chain transfers via CCIP, data feed integration, and Chainlink Runtime Environment (CRE) operations. By using these skills, developers can prompt agents to generate Solidity code, manage workflows, and perform local testing for decentralized applications.

Highlights:
- Implements standardized Agent Skills specification for AI agent interoperability.
- Enables CCIP cross-chain sends, monitoring, and local testing workflows.
- Provides multi-chain Data Feeds integration and CRE runtime operations.

## tlehman/litprog-skill

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/tlehman/litprog-skill
GitHub: https://github.com/tlehman/litprog-skill
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 81
Literate programming skill — weave code and documentation across agents

The litprog-skill is a Claude Code extension that implements Donald Knuth's literate programming paradigm by transforming codebases into human-readable narratives. It works by generating a .lit.md file that weaves together prose, Mermaid diagrams, and LaTeX math with the original source code. The tool includes a tangler to extract runnable files and a reverse-sync engine to ensure the documentation stays updated when source files are edited. This approach helps developers document architectural decisions and facilitates easier onboarding through structured, essay-like code explanations.

Highlights:
- Generates .lit.md files combining prose, diagrams, and source code
- Extracts runnable source files from documentation using a tangler
- Automatically syncs source file edits back to the literate document

---

## joeynyc/hermes-skins

GitHub: https://github.com/joeynyc/hermes-skins
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 76
Community CLI skins and themes for Hermes terminal UI

Hermes Skins is a collection of visual themes for the Hermes CLI agent that allows users to customize the terminal's appearance. It uses YAML configuration files to modify elements like banner colors, ASCII art, spinner verbs, and branding text without altering the agent's behavior. Users can apply these skins via session commands or permanent configuration files. The project includes a wide variety of themed presets ranging from retro computing aesthetics to pop-culture references.

Highlights:
- Customizes colors, ASCII art, and spinner verbs via YAML
- Supports partial skins that inherit values from the default theme
- Includes numerous themed presets like Vault-Tec, Skynet, and DOS

## sanchomuzax/hermes-webui

GitHub: https://github.com/sanchomuzax/hermes-webui
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 57
Process monitoring and configuration dashboard for Hermes

Hermes WebUI is a process monitoring and configuration dashboard designed to provide a visual interface for the Hermes Agent. It functions as a non-intrusive polling bridge that reads from the agent's SQLite database and YAML files to display real-time activity without requiring modifications to the core agent code. Users can monitor session histories, track costs, inspect skill source code, and manage scheduled cron jobs through a responsive, themeable web interface. The system ensures security by providing read-only access to environment variables and requiring token-based authentication for access.

Highlights:
- Real-time activity monitoring with session tracking and cost distribution
- Full conversation history inspection with FTS5 search capabilities
- Read-only configuration viewing and scheduled cron job management

## AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC

GitHub: https://github.com/AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 56
Hermes Agent ported to Claude Code Channel — 46 native skills, no OAuth, no external process

hermes-CCC is a port of the Hermes Agent framework specifically designed for the Claude Code native skill system. It integrates the Hermes brain architecture directly into the Claude Code execution context, eliminating the need for external processes or OAuth authentication. The project implements 46 core skills across categories such as MLOps, software development, and research, including specialized tools for RL training and vector database management. This allows developers to utilize Hermes-style memory, personas, and task routing while remaining within a unified terminal environment.

Highlights:
- Port of Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills to CCC
- Runs natively in Claude Code without separate processes or OAuth
- Includes MLOps tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and RL training

## esaradev/icarus-plugin

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/esaradev/icarus-plugin
GitHub: https://github.com/esaradev/icarus-plugin
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 51
Self-memory and replacement models — remember your work, train your replacement

Icarus is a Hermes plugin designed to create self-improving agents through shared memory and automated model fine-tuning. It captures agent decisions, session outcomes, and structured notes into a markdown-based "fabric" directory that can be optionally managed via Obsidian. This data is then used to extract high-quality training pairs for fine-tuning cheaper replacement models via Together AI. The system includes a full pipeline for evaluating candidate models against base versions and switching to them once performance thresholds are met.

Highlights:
- Cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based fabric directories
- Automated training data extraction with decision-quality tagging
- End-to-end pipeline for fine-tuning and evaluating replacement models

---

## OnlyTerp/hermes-optimization-guide

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/OnlyTerp/hermes-optimization-guide
GitHub: https://github.com/OnlyTerp/hermes-optimization-guide
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 51
Performance optimization guide for Hermes deployments

The Hermes Optimization Guide is a comprehensive technical resource and toolkit designed to move Hermes Agent deployments from basic installation to production-ready environments. It provides a 21-part curriculum alongside runnable artifacts, including a one-command VPS bootstrap script, hardened systemd units, and pre-configured Caddy templates for secure reverse proxying. Users can implement opinionated configurations for cost-optimization, security hardening, and multi-platform integration across 16 different communication gateways. The project also includes 13 installable skills and reference architectures for scaling from local homelabs to professional agency deployments.

Highlights:
- One-command VPS bootstrap script for automated production-ready deployment.
- Thirteen installable skills and five pre-tuned configuration templates.
- Reference architectures for integrating MCP servers and remote sandboxes.

## chigwell/skilldock.io

GitHub: https://github.com/chigwell/skilldock.io
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 50
Registry of reusable AI skills based on AgentSkills specification

SkillDock is a registry and SDK for managing reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification. It provides a Python client and CLI that interact with an OpenAPI-driven backend to discover, install, and version agent capabilities. The platform supports a commerce layer for buying and selling skills using Stripe or TON blockchain payments. Developers can package local skill folders, manage dependencies, and publish immutable releases with metadata defined in Markdown frontmatter.

Highlights:
- OpenAPI-driven Python SDK and CLI for skill lifecycle management
- Supports skill monetization via Stripe and TON blockchain integration
- Automated dependency resolution and local installation of AI skills

## howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness
GitHub: https://github.com/howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 46
Meta-harness for Hermes Agent — meta-optimization with arxiv paper reference

Hermes Agent Meta-Harness is a standalone outer-loop optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes agents on verifiable coding benchmarks. It treats the agent as an execution backend and searches for optimal harness configurations—the code governing context collection and retrieval—rather than modifying model weights. The system automates benchmark orchestration, archive analysis, and frontier tracking to identify high-performing harness candidates. It currently supports TBLite and TB2 benchmarks, providing structured mutation and search capabilities to refine agentic workflows.

Highlights:
- Optimizes harness code through an automated outer-loop search process
- Orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluation via Hermes Agent
- Tracks performance frontiers with structured candidate mutation and comparison

---

## black-forest-labs/skills

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/black-forest-labs/skills
GitHub: https://github.com/black-forest-labs/skills
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 44
Official FLUX image generation skills — prompting guidelines and API integration

This project provides official image generation skills for FLUX models, offering standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns following the agentskills.io specification. It functions as a technical resource for developers to implement text-to-image and image-to-image workflows through structured JSON prompting, hex color specification, and multi-reference editing. The repository includes a model selection guide for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 variants and can be integrated directly into Claude Code as a plugin. Users can access documented API patterns for asynchronous polling, webhook verification, and rate limit management.

Highlights:
- Standardized prompting guidelines for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 model families
- API integration patterns for polling, webhooks, and error handling
- Support for JSON structured prompting and precise hex color specification

## unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser
GitHub: https://github.com/unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 44
AI-native browser built for autonomous agent control via MCP

Vessel is an open-source, Chromium-based browser designed specifically for autonomous agent control rather than traditional human browsing. It provides a persistent runtime where external agent harnesses like Hermes Agent can drive workflows while humans monitor and intervene through a visible supervisory interface. The browser maintains durable state through named sessions, annotated checkpoints, and an action undo feature that rolls back the environment to a state prior to the last agent action. It integrates with the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to expose browser tools, page schema inference, and structured bookmarks to AI agents.

Highlights:
- Provides a human-visible UI for auditing and supervising autonomous agents
- Maintains persistent browser state with named sessions and annotated checkpoints
- Exposes structured page schemas and metadata via MCP for agents

---

## raulvidis/hermes-android

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/raulvidis/hermes-android
GitHub: https://github.com/raulvidis/hermes-android
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 38
Android device control — bridge app + Python toolset for mobile automation

Hermes-android is a remote automation bridge that enables Hermes agents to control Android devices through a Kotlin-based mobile app and a Python toolset. The system establishes an outbound WebSocket connection from the phone to a server, bypassing the need for port forwarding or USB cables via a 6-character pairing code. It provides 36 specialized tools for UI interaction, including screen reading, gesture execution, and system-level actions like making calls or sending SMS. This integration allows agents to perform autonomous mobile tasks such as checking notifications, managing contacts, and navigating third-party applications.

Highlights:
- Executes 36 tools for UI navigation, media control, and system actions
- Uses AccessibilityService for screen reading and coordinate-based gesture automation
- Connects via WebSocket relay to work behind NAT and firewalls

---

## Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory
GitHub: https://github.com/Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 34
Meta-skill plugin that watches workflows and auto-generates reusable skills

Skill Factory is a meta-skill for Hermes Agent that automates the creation of reusable skills by monitoring user workflows. It functions by tracking session patterns in the background and proposing structured skill definitions when it detects repeatable sequences. Users can generate both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds directly from these proposals via slash commands or natural language. This tool streamlines the expansion of the Hermes ecosystem by turning manual environment setups and development tasks into permanent, executable assets.

Highlights:
- Automatically detects and proposes reusable skills from active workflows
- Generates both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds
- Provides slash commands to manage, list, and save detected patterns

---

## plur-ai/plur

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/plur-ai/plur
GitHub: https://github.com/plur-ai/plur
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 31
Shared memory layer with open engram YAML format for multi-agent systems

PLUR is a persistent, local-first memory layer designed to synchronize agent corrections, preferences, and conventions across different AI tools and sessions. It stores knowledge as plain YAML files called engrams, using an activation model that strengthens frequently used facts while allowing stale information to naturally decay. The system employs a hybrid search approach combining BM25 and embeddings to inject relevant context into agent prompts without cloud API calls. It integrates directly with Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and OpenClaw to automatically capture and recall learnings during active conversations.

Highlights:
- Stores persistent agent memory locally in open YAML engram format
- Uses hybrid BM25 and embedding search for zero-cost local retrieval
- Automates memory injection and extraction for Hermes and MCP tools

---

## keepnotes-ai/keep

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/keepnotes-ai/keep
GitHub: https://github.com/keepnotes-ai/keep
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 31
Reflective memory for AI agents

Keep is a reflective memory system for AI agents that enables long-term learning and contextual awareness. It processes diverse inputs—including URLs, codebases, and media files—by summarizing, embedding, and organizing them into a navigable graph of "edge tags." The system automatically surfaces relevant past learnings and commitments during agent sessions to improve decision-making. It supports fully local operation via Ollama or MLX, as well as cloud-hosted and API-based configurations.

Highlights:
- Indexes codebases, documents, and media with automatic summarization and OCR
- Uses edge tags to create navigable, bidirectional relationships between notes
- Provides MCP and CLI interfaces for seamless agent tool integration

---

## runtimenoteslabs/gladiator

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/runtimenoteslabs/gladiator
GitHub: https://github.com/runtimenoteslabs/gladiator
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 29
Autonomous AI companies competing for GitHub stars — agent-vs-agent arena

Gladiator is an autonomous agent arena that pits two AI companies against each other to compete for GitHub stars on identical repositories. The system uses Paperclip for orchestration and Hermes Agent workers to execute tasks according to distinct corporate strategies: growth-focused Blitz versus quality-focused Craft. A real-time dashboard visualizes the competition through scoreboards, task boards, and audit trails. Upon completion, the project demonstrates cross-company skill transfer by merging the rival entities and their learned memories.

Highlights:
- Orchestrates 9 autonomous agents across two competing AI companies
- Uses Paperclip and Hermes Agent for task execution and learning
- Real-time dashboard visualizes agent tasks, code, and scoreboard metrics

---

## AxDSan/mnemosyne

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/AxDSan/mnemosyne
GitHub: https://github.com/AxDSan/mnemosyne
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 29
The Zero-Dependency, Sub-Millisecond AI Memory System for Hermes Agents

Mnemosyne is a local-first memory system designed to provide persistent storage for Hermes Agents without relying on cloud services or external databases. It utilizes a SQLite backend with native vector search and full-text search to manage conversations, preferences, and knowledge in-process. The system implements a three-tier BEAM architecture that handles working memory, episodic memory, and temporary scratchpads while supporting automatic consolidation through sleep cycles. By operating entirely on-device, it eliminates network latency and API costs while ensuring data privacy and offline functionality.

Highlights:
- Sub-millisecond local retrieval using SQLite-vec and FTS5 hybrid search
- Three-tier BEAM architecture for working, episodic, and scratchpad memory
- Zero-dependency installation with native Hermes Agent plugin integration

---

Stars: 28
17 specialized agents for research, planning, implementation, quality, and infrastructure

Hermes is a multi-agent orchestration system built for OpenCode AI to manage the full software development lifecycle. It utilizes a master orchestrator to route tasks through a pipeline of 17 specialized agents categorized into research, planning, implementation, quality, documentation, and infrastructure roles. The system enforces mandatory quality gates and security audits while maintaining context across different LLM models including GPT, Gemini, and Claude. It supports automated workflows for feature development, bug fixing, refactoring, and performance optimization.

Highlights:
- Coordinates 17 specialized agents across distinct development phases
- Implements mandatory quality gates for code reviews and testing
- Integrates multiple LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google

## Euraika-Labs/pan-ui

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Euraika-Labs/pan-ui
GitHub: https://github.com/Euraika-Labs/pan-ui
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 27
Self-hosted AI workspace with chat, skills, extensions, memory, profiles, and runtime controls

Pan is a self-hosted web dashboard and workspace designed to provide a graphical interface for the Hermes Agent runtime. It functions as a Next.js server that bridges the browser to the Hermes API, automatically managing the agent gateway and providing a centralized control plane. Users can manage isolated profiles, browse and install skills from skills.sh, and monitor real-time tool executions through a streaming chat interface. The platform also includes dedicated management views for Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations, memory inspection, and system-wide audit trails.

Highlights:
- Chat-first workspace with SSE streaming and tool execution timelines
- Integrated marketplace for skills.sh, MCP servers, and plugins
- Isolated profile management for separate sessions, memory, and keys
