## ReinaMacCredy/maestro

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/ReinaMacCredy/maestro
GitHub: https://github.com/ReinaMacCredy/maestro
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 25
Harness for long-running AI agents — structured memory, cross-feature learning, plan-approve-execute

Maestro is a local-first conductor for multi-agent software engineering that enables persistent state management across separate agent sessions. It uses a CLI to store missions, features, and memory in a local .maestro directory, removing the need for external databases or servers. The tool allows human operators to coordinate multiple terminals by providing structured handoff briefs and cross-feature learning. This approach ensures that project context and agent progress remain disciplined and inspectable through a dedicated Mission Control TUI.

Highlights:
- Local-first state management using on-disk .maestro directory and JSON snapshots
- Structured mission planning with milestones, features, and validation assertions
- Native handoff launches that generate markdown briefs for fresh agent runs

---

## Abruptive/Ankh.md

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Abruptive/Ankh.md
GitHub: https://github.com/Abruptive/Ankh.md
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 25
TAW Agent framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents

Ankh.md is an open-source framework designed to transform a single Hermes Agent into multiple specialized, project-specific instances. It works by detecting a local `.agent/` directory and dynamically overriding the default `hermes` command with a scoped agent configuration. This allows developers to maintain unique instructions, skills, and memories for different codebases or workflows without cross-project interference.

Highlights:
- Per-folder scoping with local configuration and skills
- Seamlessly switches between default and scoped Hermes agents
- Supports project-local memories, tools, and custom instructions

---

## pyrate-llama/hermes-ui

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/pyrate-llama/hermes-ui
GitHub: https://github.com/pyrate-llama/hermes-ui
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 25
Glassmorphic web interface for Hermes Agent — your self-hosted AI assistant

Hermes UI is a glassmorphic web interface for the self-hosted Hermes Agent, providing a visual workspace for AI interaction and system management. It operates as a single-file React application served via a lightweight Python proxy, requiring no complex build steps or external dependencies. The interface surfaces advanced agent features such as real-time log streaming, MCP tool browsing, and a dedicated artifact panel for rendering HTML, SVG, and PDF outputs. Users can manage model providers, monitor system memory, and execute shell commands through an integrated terminal environment.

Highlights:
- Single-file React architecture with no npm dependencies required
- Real-time artifact panel for live HTML, SVG, and PDF previews
- Integrated management for MCP tools, cron jobs, and memory files

---

## tiann/execplan-skill

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/tiann/execplan-skill
GitHub: https://github.com/tiann/execplan-skill
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 24
Complex multi-step task execution with checkpoints and recovery

ExecPlan is an Agent Skill designed to help AI coding agents autonomously manage complex, long-running implementation tasks. It utilizes a structured execution plan that allows users to review and approve the approach before implementation starts. The system tracks progress in real-time and provides rollback procedures for safe recovery. It is compatible with tools such as Claude Code, Cursor, and Goose.

Highlights:
- Autonomous execution for long-running tasks
- Real-time tracking of progress and decisions
- Built-in rollback procedures for safe recovery

---

## roli-lpci/lintlang

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/roli-lpci/lintlang
GitHub: https://github.com/roli-lpci/lintlang
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 24
Static linter for AI agent configs, tool descriptions, and system prompts. HERM v1.1 scoring.

lintlang is a static analysis tool designed to identify language-level failures in AI agent configurations, system prompts, and tool definitions. It employs seven structural detectors to catch issues like ambiguous tool descriptions and missing termination conditions without making any LLM calls. By running locally or in CI pipelines, the tool provides immediate feedback and concrete fix suggestions to prevent agent loops and incorrect tool selection. It supports multiple file formats including YAML, JSON, and Markdown, ensuring prompt quality before runtime testing.

Highlights:
- Identifies structural language bugs using seven specialized static detectors.
- Operates with zero LLM calls, zero latency, and no API keys.
- Provides automated quality gating for CI/CD pipelines with verdict-based exits.

---

## cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill
GitHub: https://github.com/cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 22
Progressive MCP client with three-layer lazy loading — validates agentskills.io pattern

Agentic-MCP-Skill is an experimental MCP client designed to optimize token usage through a three-layer progressive disclosure pattern. It utilizes a socket-based daemon architecture to manage persistent connections to MCP servers rather than loading full tool definitions into the AI context. By loading metadata, tool lists, and specific schemas only when required, the system can reduce token consumption by up to 86% compared to standard loading methods. The project currently supports hot-reloading configurations and provides a CLI for interacting with servers like Playwright and Filesystem.

Highlights:
- Three-layer lazy loading reduces AI context token consumption
- Socket-based daemon maintains persistent MCP server connections
- Supports hot-reloading server configurations without restarting the daemon

---

## robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus
GitHub: https://github.com/robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 21
Multi-provider web search (Serper, Tavily, Exa, Querit, Perplexity) with auto-routing

Hermes Web Search Plus is a multi-provider search plugin for the Hermes Agent that enables intelligent information retrieval from ten different search engines. The system uses an auto-routing engine to select the most appropriate provider, such as Exa for semantic discovery or Serper for news, based on the specific intent of the user's query. It features adaptive fallback logic to skip providers on cooldown and includes a dedicated tool for extracting clean markdown content from URLs. Users can further refine results through time-range filtering, domain restrictions, and specialized deep-research modes for complex synthesis.

Highlights:
- Auto-routes queries across 10 providers including Brave, Exa, and Tavily
- Supports deep research and cross-document analysis via Exa integration
- Includes URL extraction tool for converting web content to markdown

---

## 0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes
GitHub: https://github.com/0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 21
Migration tool from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent

openclaw-to-hermes is a migration utility designed to transition users from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent. It automates the transfer of agent personas, workspace files, model configurations, and credentials between the two ecosystems. The tool addresses specific limitations found in earlier migration methods, such as incorrect authentication formatting and incomplete workspace detection. It provides a safe migration path through dry-run modes and automated backup creation.

Highlights:
- Migrates agent personas, memories, skills, and workspace files
- Maps complex OpenClaw model configurations to Hermes YAML format
- Handles credential transfers from local and system-level environment files

## armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge
GitHub: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 17
Converts repos, docs, and developer discourse into agentskills.io-compliant skills

Skill Forge is a development tool that converts source code, documentation, and developer discourse into verified instruction files for AI agents. It uses Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) extraction to map function signatures and types directly to specific file paths and line numbers, ensuring agents use real APIs instead of hallucinating parameters. The tool generates version-pinned skills compliant with the agentskills.io specification, allowing for immutable audit trails via a provenance map. It supports automated workflows for creating, testing, and exporting skills to platforms like Cursor, Claude, and Copilot.

Highlights:
- Maps AI instructions to specific source code file and line numbers
- Generates agentskills.io-compliant markdown and JSON instruction sets
- Supports version-pinned skill management for library updates and migrations

---

## 42-evey/hermes-plugins

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/42-evey/hermes-plugins
GitHub: https://github.com/42-evey/hermes-plugins
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 16
Goal management, inter-agent bridge, model selection, and cost control plugins

Evey's Hermes Plugins is a collection of 23 custom extensions designed to enhance the autonomy, observability, and cost-efficiency of the Hermes Agent framework. The suite implements specialized logic for multi-model decision debates, smart task routing with fallback chains, and structured telemetry logging. These tools enable agents to manage their own goals, perform self-correction loops, and consolidate long-term memory into vector storage. Additionally, the project includes a bidirectional bridge for Claude Code and budget enforcement via Langfuse integration.

Highlights:
- Autonomous planning, reflection, and multi-model consensus debate tools
- Smart model routing with sensitivity filters and four-model fallback chains
- Automated memory consolidation into Qdrant vectors and identity evolution

## TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw
GitHub: https://github.com/TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 16
Hermes Agent sandboxed with hardware-level enforcement

HermesClaw is a deployment framework that runs the Hermes Agent within NVIDIA’s OpenShell sandbox to provide hardware-level security enforcement. It utilizes kernel-level mechanisms like Landlock LSM and Seccomp BPF to restrict network egress, filesystem access, and system calls. The environment supports persistent memory, self-improving skills, and multi-agent delegation while maintaining strict isolation from the host OS. Users can toggle between security policy presets to enable or disable external integrations like Telegram, Discord, and web searching.

Highlights:
- Hardware-enforced sandboxing via NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level syscall blocking
- Pre-configured security policies ranging from strict inference to permissive access
- Integrated library of 40+ tools including terminal, vision, and messaging

---

## amanning3390/hermeshub

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/amanning3390/hermeshub
GitHub: https://github.com/amanning3390/hermeshub
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 15
Community skill browsing, search, and one-click installation hub

HermesHub is a curated skills registry and marketplace for the Hermes Agent ecosystem that provides a centralized platform for discovering and installing AI capabilities. The system utilizes an automated security scanner with over 65 rules to vet skills for threats like data exfiltration and prompt injection before they are listed. It supports the agentskills.io open standard and features a creator marketplace where developers can monetize skills using crypto-based payment protocols. Users can manage their agent's capabilities through a command-line interface for browsing, searching, and one-click installation.

Highlights:
- Automated security scanning for data exfiltration and prompt injection
- Supports x402 and Micropayment protocols for premium skill monetization
- Agent-to-agent feedback system with proof-of-use reviews and trust scores

## nativ3ai/hermes-agent-camel

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/nativ3ai/hermes-agent-camel
GitHub: https://github.com/nativ3ai/hermes-agent-camel
Category: Forks & Derivatives
Stars: 12
Hermes with integrated CaMeL trust boundaries for safer autonomous execution

This project is a specialized fork of Hermes Agent that integrates CaMeL trust-boundary principles to defend against prompt-injection attacks during autonomous execution. It implements a security layer that monitors or enforces policies to block unauthorized sensitive actions when the agent is operating under untrusted contexts. Users can explicitly opt into these protections using specific CLI flags while maintaining access to the core Hermes features like autonomous skill creation and multi-platform messaging. The system supports various runtime modes, including a monitor mode for tracing policy decisions and an enforcement mode for active threat mitigation.

Highlights:
- Implements CaMeL trust-boundary principles for prompt-injection defense
- Offers monitor and enforce modes for granular security control
- Maintains full compatibility with upstream Hermes Agent features

---

## 0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent
GitHub: https://github.com/0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 11
Nix package and NixOS module for reproducible Hermes deployment

nix-hermes-agent provides a Nix package and NixOS module for the declarative deployment of Hermes Agent. It allows users to manage the entire agent lifecycle—including configuration, workspace documents, and systemd services—through a single Nix configuration file. The module ensures reproducibility by rendering settings into a structured CLI configuration while keeping sensitive API keys secure via external environment files. It supports both Nix-managed declarative skills and native Hermes CLI interactive skills, providing a stable foundation for production-ready agent infrastructure.

Highlights:
- Declarative management of agent config, documents, and systemd services
- Secure secrets handling via systemd EnvironmentFiles and sops-nix integration
- Hybrid skill support for both Nix-managed and interactive CLI installs

---

## Lethe044/hermes-incident-commander

GitHub: https://github.com/Lethe044/hermes-incident-commander
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 11
Autonomous SRE agent — detects, heals, and learns from production incidents

Hermes Incident Commander is an autonomous SRE agent designed to automate the detection, diagnosis, and remediation of production infrastructure failures. It utilizes the Hermes Agent framework to monitor system vitals, classify incident severity, and execute multi-step diagnostic pipelines to identify root causes. Once a fix is applied and verified, the agent generates structured post-mortem reports and creates new prevention skills to handle similar future incidents more efficiently. The system integrates with Telegram and Discord for real-time alerting and supports cloud-native remediation via MCP servers.

Highlights:
- Automates incident triage, root cause analysis, and remediation actions.
- Generates persistent prevention skills and post-incident reports autonomously.
- Supports parallel subagent spawning for multi-layer service investigations.

## Christabel337/job-scout-agent

GitHub: https://github.com/Christabel337/job-scout-agent
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 11
Autonomous job hunting — scans listings, writes cover letters, tracks applications

Job Scout Agent is an automated job search tool designed to streamline the discovery and tracking of employment opportunities. The agent utilizes Hermes Agent capabilities to fetch live listings from job board APIs, score them against user-defined profiles, and perform company research. It organizes findings into structured local directories and maintains a CSV tracker for easy integration with Google Sheets. This system eliminates manual searching by providing a daily summary of relevant roles with direct application links.

Highlights:
- Fetches live job listings using browser-based API integration
- Scores roles against skills, salary, and location preferences
- Maintains an automated CSV tracker for application management

## Codename-11/hermes-relay

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Codename-11/hermes-relay
GitHub: https://github.com/Codename-11/hermes-relay
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 10
Hermes Companion — Android app for Hermes agent platform. Chat, terminal, and device control over WSS.

Hermes-Relay is a companion suite that enables remote control and interaction with a server-deployed Hermes agent via Android and desktop interfaces. It uses a WebSocket Secure (WSS) relay and a dedicated server plugin to bridge the gap between a remote agent's brain and a user's local hardware. The Android app allows the agent to read screens and perform device actions like swiping or typing, while the desktop CLI enables the remote agent to execute local terminal commands and access files. This setup provides a unified experience where a single remote agent can act on multiple local surfaces without requiring SSH.

Highlights:
- Native Android app for chat, voice, and remote device control
- Desktop CLI for running remote Hermes agents as local processes
- Bi-directional tool execution allowing remote agents to run local commands

## rodmarkun/anihermes

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/rodmarkun/anihermes
GitHub: https://github.com/rodmarkun/anihermes
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 9
Local anime server and tracker via natural language for Hermes Agent

AniHermes is a natural language anime media server and tracker built on the Hermes Agent framework. It leverages LLM orchestration to manage downloads via qBittorrent, track series progress, and organize local libraries through conversational interfaces like Telegram, Discord, or CLI. The system utilizes agentic capabilities such as vision analysis for identifying anime from screenshots and automated CRON jobs for scheduling weekly episode downloads. It integrates directly with Anilist for watchlist synchronization and provides parallel search functionality across multiple sources like SubsPlease and Nyaa.

Highlights:
- Natural language control for downloads, tracking, and library management
- Vision-based anime identification from screenshots using Hermes Agent
- Automated weekly episode tracking via NL-defined CRON jobs

It utilizes cron-based workflows to ingest AI research from arXiv and track trending infrastructure repositories on GitHub. The system automatically generates structured reports on compute risk levels, infrastructure trends, and cost projections. It serves as a template for developers to deploy long-running, headless agentic systems for operational intelligence.

## teknium1/hermes-miniverse

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/teknium1/hermes-miniverse
GitHub: https://github.com/teknium1/hermes-miniverse
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 8
Bridge Hermes to Miniverse pixel worlds — agent embodiment in virtual environments

Hermes-miniverse is a bridge that enables Hermes Agents to inhabit and interact within Miniverse pixel worlds. It functions through a three-part architecture consisting of a standalone bridge daemon, a gateway hook for state broadcasting, and a specific skill that allows the agent to consciously navigate the environment. This integration allows agents to maintain a visual presence, update their status in real-time, and engage in bidirectional communication with other AI entities. Users can deploy multiple bridges simultaneously to populate a virtual world with specialized Hermes instances.

Highlights:
- Connects Hermes Agents to Miniverse pixel-based virtual environments
- Synchronizes live agent states including working, thinking, and idle
- Enables bidirectional messaging between Hermes and other world agents

## bigph00t/hermescraft

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/bigph00t/hermescraft
GitHub: https://github.com/bigph00t/hermescraft
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 8
Embodied AI companion for Minecraft — persistent memory, vision, learning, multi-agent

HermesCraft is an embodied AI framework that integrates Hermes agents into Minecraft as persistent, interactive players. It connects the Hermes agent stack to a Mineflayer bot body through a custom Minecraft CLI and HTTP API, allowing agents to perceive and interact with the game world. Agents can function as individual companions or as part of multi-agent civilizations with private messaging and social memory. The system emphasizes fair-play perception by restricting agent knowledge to line-of-sight visuals and directional audio rather than using privileged game data.

Highlights:
- Embodied agents with persistent memory, identity, and tool-use capabilities
- Fair-play perception system using line-of-sight filtering and spatial mapping
- Multi-agent social features including public chat and private messaging

## metantonio/hermes-wsl-ubuntu

GitHub: https://github.com/metantonio/hermes-wsl-ubuntu
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 8
Step-by-step WSL2 Ubuntu setup guide for Windows users

This project provides a comprehensive setup guide and automation script for running Hermes Agent locally on Windows via WSL2, Linux, or macOS. It integrates llama.cpp for model inference with Qwen3.5 GGUF models, utilizing CUDA or Metal acceleration for optimized performance. The guide covers the full stack including the Camofox browser server for web-browsing tasks and the Hermes HUDUI for a graphical user interface. Users can deploy a production-ready agentic environment with local LLM support and OpenAI-compatible API endpoints.

Highlights:
- Automated installation script for Hermes Agent, llama.cpp, and Camofox
- Supports GPU acceleration via CUDA for WSL2 and Metal for macOS
- Configures Qwen3.5 GGUF models for local inference and web browsing

## kaminocorp/hermes-alpha

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/kaminocorp/hermes-alpha
GitHub: https://github.com/kaminocorp/hermes-alpha
Category: Forks & Derivatives
Stars: 8
Cloud-deployed Hermes with pre-configured templates and managed infrastructure

Hermes Alpha is an experimental framework designed to test if a stock AI agent can autonomously bootstrap and manage a bug bounty system from first principles. The architecture utilizes a two-agent loop where a persistent 'Overseer' agent builds, deploys, and refines an ephemeral 'Hunter' agent tasked with finding software vulnerabilities. By providing the agent with only a Linux terminal and an identity document rather than custom tools, the project evaluates the self-improvement capabilities of the Hermes model. The system integrates with Fly.io for managed infrastructure and uses Elephantasm for long-term memory streams.

Highlights:
- Autonomous two-agent loop for self-improving vulnerability research
- Web-based terminal interface with persistent WebSocket sessions
- Managed deployment of sub-agents via Fly.io infrastructure

---

## FahrenheitResearch/hermes-weather-plugin

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/FahrenheitResearch/hermes-weather-plugin
GitHub: https://github.com/FahrenheitResearch/hermes-weather-plugin
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 7
NWS-grade model imagery, NEXRAD radar, and verified meteorological calculations. Pure Rust.

The Hermes Weather Plugin provides a suite of 13 meteorological tools for the Hermes Agent ecosystem, enabling real-time access to observations, forecasts, and high-fidelity model imagery. It utilizes a Python-first architecture that offloads heavy computational tasks, such as NEXRAD radar rendering and ECAPE calculations, to high-performance Rust backends. The plugin features an automated bootstrap process that compiles necessary Rust binaries on first use, ensuring a streamlined setup for complex weather analysis. Users can retrieve verified model soundings, severe weather outlooks from the SPC, and global data via Open-Meteo integration.

Highlights:
- Thirteen tools for NWS forecasts, radar imagery, and severe weather alerts
- Rust-backed meteorological calculations for ECAPE, soundings, and parcel profiles
- Automated bootstrap system for compiling and caching required Rust binaries

---

## supermodeltools/bigiron

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/supermodeltools/bigiron
GitHub: https://github.com/supermodeltools/bigiron
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 7
AI-native SDLC — Hermes Agent + Supermodel code graph, graph-gated at every phase

Big Iron is an experimental AI-native software development system that automates the full development lifecycle through a graph-gated workflow. It integrates Hermes Agent with the Supermodel Code Graph API to replace traditional file-loading context with structural intelligence, including call graphs and dependency maps. The system executes an eight-phase process—from planning to autonomous health checks—using specific skills and quality gates to ensure architectural alignment. While now deprecated in favor of the Supermodel CLI, it serves as a prototype for autonomous, graph-grounded code generation and refactoring.

Highlights:
- Executes an autonomous eight-phase SDLC from planning to health checks
- Uses Supermodel MCP to provide Hermes with structural code graphs
- Enforces architectural quality through automated graph-diff phase gates

## rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent
GitHub: https://github.com/rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 7
Windows desktop agent — 100 tools, GUI, local models, TTS, ComfyUI. No install, no Docker.

Portable Hermes Agent is a Windows desktop implementation of the Hermes Agent designed for non-technical users to run AI agents without installation or admin rights. It utilizes a Tkinter GUI and an embedded Python environment to bridge local models via LM Studio or cloud providers like OpenRouter. The system features a workflow engine for automating multi-step pipelines and a dynamic tool maker for creating custom API wrappers at runtime. It also integrates portable servers for text-to-speech, music generation, and ComfyUI image generation.

Highlights:
- 100 tools including local model management and GPU monitoring
- No-install deployment requiring no admin rights or Docker
- Integrated workflow engine for scheduled and conditional automation

## 42-evey/evey-setup

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/42-evey/evey-setup
GitHub: https://github.com/42-evey/evey-setup
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 7
Get a hermes-agent stack running in minutes — free models, 29 plugins, zero cost

Evey Setup is a deployment toolkit designed to bootstrap a complete autonomous AI agent environment based on the Hermes Agent framework. It uses a multi-phase shell script to orchestrate a containerized stack including model routing via LiteLLM, local GPU inference through Ollama, and vector memory with Qdrant. The installer provides three service tiers—Base, Services, and Full—allowing users to scale from a minimal agent to a production-ready system with monitoring and workflow automation. It integrates 29 community plugins and pre-configures free model routing to enable zero-cost operation.

Highlights:
- Deploys Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant via Docker
- Includes 29 community plugins for memory, social, and quality validation
- Automates configuration for free model routing and local GPU inference

## PederHP/skillsdotnet

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/PederHP/skillsdotnet
GitHub: https://github.com/PederHP/skillsdotnet
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 6
C# / .NET implementation of agentskills.io with MCP integration

SkillsDotNet is a C# implementation of the agentskills.io specification that enables the distribution and discovery of agent capabilities via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It uses a "context-as-progressive-disclosure" approach where agents initially load small frontmatter summaries and only fetch full instructions or supporting files on demand. The library integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI to provide a standardized tool for models to load skills during active chat sessions. It includes built-in support for discovering skills from common agent directories like Claude, Cursor, and Copilot.

Highlights:
- Implements agentskills.io specification using the C# MCP SDK
- Provides progressive disclosure of skill context to save tokens
- Supports automatic skill discovery from popular agent vendor directories

---

## xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker
GitHub: https://github.com/xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 6
Simple Docker sandbox image for Hermes Agent

This project provides a minimal Docker sandbox image designed to package and deploy Hermes Agent. It utilizes the official upstream installation script to set up the agent and includes mini-swe-agent within the container. The image supports multi-arch publishing and allows for persistent state management by mounting the agent's home directory. Users can easily build specific versions using build arguments to target different Hermes branches or tags.

Highlights:
- Includes mini-swe-agent and official Hermes installation
- Supports persistent state via mounted home directories
- Allows building specific Hermes tags using build arguments

---

## Ridwannurudeen/hermes-council

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Ridwannurudeen/hermes-council
GitHub: https://github.com/Ridwannurudeen/hermes-council
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 6
Adversarial multi-perspective council MCP server for decision-making

Hermes-council is an MCP server designed to provide adversarial, multi-perspective decision-making for the hermes-agent ecosystem. It functions by orchestrating five distinct personas—Advocate, Skeptic, Oracle, Contrarian, and Arbiter—to debate complex questions through structured deliberation. The system produces calibrated verdicts with confidence scores and evidence links, and it can be used to generate DPO preference pairs for Reinforcement Learning training.

Highlights:
- Five distinct intellectual personas for adversarial deliberation
- Generates DPO preference pairs for RL training
- Supports custom persona configuration via YAML

## mudrii/hermes-agent-docs

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/mudrii/hermes-agent-docs
GitHub: https://github.com/mudrii/hermes-agent-docs
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 6
Community documentation covering deployment patterns and v0.2.0+ workflows

Hermes Agent is an autonomous AI system designed for persistent learning and multi-platform deployment across various messaging and development environments. It utilizes a closed learning loop to create reusable skills from experience, manage long-term memory via SQLite, and maintain a consistent user model across sessions. The agent supports concurrent tool execution and integrates with dozens of model providers and messaging gateways, including Telegram, Discord, and Slack. Its architecture allows for extensible functionality through a plugin system and Model Context Protocol (MCP) server management.

Highlights:
- Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement based on task performance
- Unified messaging gateway supporting over 15 communication platforms
- Multi-provider flexibility with support for Nous Portal, Anthropic, and OpenAI

---

## amanning3390/flowstate-qmd

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/amanning3390/flowstate-qmd
GitHub: https://github.com/amanning3390/flowstate-qmd
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 5
Anticipatory memory with RAG and vector search — Hermes 2026 Hackathon entry

FlowState-QMD is a local-first memory layer designed to provide coding agents with immediate project context from markdown documentation. It utilizes a SQLite-based vector store and an anticipatory cache that prefetches relevant documents by monitoring active agent session logs. This approach allows agents to access design docs, ADRs, and changelogs before initiating a reactive search loop. The system integrates via an MCP server and supports local execution using GGUF models and node-llama-cpp.

Highlights:
- Anticipatory cache prefetches context by watching agent session logs
- Local-first storage using SQLite-vec and GGUF models via node-llama-cpp
- MCP-native server with automated configuration for major coding agent clients

---

## hxsteric/mercury

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/hxsteric/mercury
GitHub: https://github.com/hxsteric/mercury
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 5
Blockchain cash flow analyzer — multi-chain analysis, fraud detection, WebGL dashboard

Mercury is a blockchain cash flow analyzer and Hermes Agent skill designed to provide transparency into wallet activities across Ethereum, Base, and Solana. It utilizes parallel subagents to fetch transaction data, identify six specific fraud patterns, and generate AI-driven trading strategies based on wallet behavior. The project features a multi-layer WebGL and Canvas dashboard for real-time visualization of inflows, outflows, and risk metrics. It operates as a standalone tool or an integrated Hermes skill for automated multi-chain investigations.

Highlights:
- Analyzes transaction flows across Ethereum, Base, and Solana chains
- Detects six fraud patterns including mixer interactions and dust attacks
- Visualizes data via interactive WebGL and Canvas-based dashboards

---

## 42-evey/evey-bridge-plugin

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/42-evey/evey-bridge-plugin
GitHub: https://github.com/42-evey/evey-bridge-plugin
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 4
Claude Code and Hermes context sharing bridge — auto-checks messages, Mother Mode loop

The Evey Bridge Plugin is a technical integration that connects Claude Code with the Evey hermes-agent stack. It functions by automatically checking for messages or tasks from the bridge and injecting them as context into every prompt submission. The plugin includes specialized commands for running a 'Mother Mode' improvement loop and monitoring system health, including wallet and cron status. It is designed specifically for users running the Evey stack at a designated local directory.

Highlights:
- Injects Evey messages and tasks into Claude Code prompts
- Automates Mother Mode improvement loops via the /evey-loop command
- Monitors health, wallets, and crons with quick status checks

---

## nativ3ai/hermes-payguard

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/nativ3ai/hermes-payguard
GitHub: https://github.com/nativ3ai/hermes-payguard
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 4
Safe-by-design USDC and x402 payment plugin for Hermes Agent

Hermes PayGuard is a security-focused plugin that enables Hermes Agents to handle USDC and x402 payments through a gated execution model. It separates payment preparation from execution by requiring an out-of-band human approval stamp for transfers exceeding user-defined policy limits. The system supports Circle developer-controlled wallets, cross-chain CCTP transfers, and automated x402 micropayments for paid HTTP fetches. This architecture ensures that while agents can stage financial intents, they cannot unilaterally move significant funds without explicit operator consent.

Highlights:
- Enforces human-in-the-loop approval for high-value USDC transfers
- Supports Circle CCTP for cross-chain USDC routing and execution
- Automates x402 micropayments below configurable policy thresholds

---

## Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill
GitHub: https://github.com/Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 4
ACP-style multi-agent delegation — routes tasks to Hermes, Codex, or Claude Code

The hermes-acp-orchestrator is a skill designed to standardize multi-agent delegation within engineering workflows. It functions by routing specific goals to specialized agents including Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while maintaining strict context isolation. The system ensures operational stability through configurable safety controls such as execution timeouts and output character limits. This tool is primarily intended for complex tasks requiring distinct phases like implementation, risk analysis, and final synthesis.

Highlights:
- Routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code.
- Prevents parent context pollution through isolated delegation workflows.
- Enforces safety via configurable timeouts and output character limits.

---

## marlandoj/zouroboros-swarm-executors

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/marlandoj/zouroboros-swarm-executors
GitHub: https://github.com/marlandoj/zouroboros-swarm-executors
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 4
Claude Code and Hermes task handoff bridge for zo-swarm-orchestrator

Zouroboros Swarm Executors is a bridge system that integrates local CLI-based AI tools into the Zo Computer swarm orchestration ecosystem. It functions by using bash bridge scripts to wrap tools like Hermes, Claude Code, and Gemini, allowing them to receive tasks via standard input and return results via standard output. This approach enables local execution of complex tasks such as security audits and web research while maintaining a shared identity and memory system across all personas. The project includes a health-check system to validate environment configurations and a registry for adding custom CLI tools as swarm members.

Highlights:
- Wraps CLI tools into AI personas with shared identity files
- Provides bridge scripts for Hermes, Claude Code, Gemini, and Codex
- Routes tasks locally instead of through remote API calls

---

## briancaffey/hermes-otel

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/briancaffey/hermes-otel
GitHub: https://github.com/briancaffey/hermes-otel
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 4
OTel Plugin for Hermes Agent

hermes-otel is an observability plugin for the Hermes Agent that enables comprehensive monitoring of agentic workflows. It automatically captures and exports LLM tool calls, model invocations, and API requests as OpenTelemetry spans to any OTLP-compatible backend. The plugin provides deep visibility into session lifecycles, including parent-child span nesting and token usage aggregation. It supports a wide range of observability platforms including Phoenix, Langfuse, SigNoz, and Grafana for tracking traces, metrics, and logs.

Highlights:
- Exports LLM tool calls and model invocations as OTel spans
- Supports multiple backends including Phoenix, Langfuse, and SigNoz
- Aggregates token usage and session I/O metrics automatically

---

## gizdusum/hermes-blockchain-oracle

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/gizdusum/hermes-blockchain-oracle
GitHub: https://github.com/gizdusum/hermes-blockchain-oracle
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 3
Solana blockchain intelligence MCP server — wallets, whales, tokens, NFTs, network health

Hermes Blockchain Oracle is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides Hermes Agent with real-time access to the Solana blockchain. It functions as a bridge between natural language queries and on-chain data by exposing specialized tools for wallet, token, and transaction analysis. The server enables users to monitor network health, track whale movements, and manage NFT portfolios through conversational interactions. By integrating directly with Solana RPC nodes, it eliminates the need for manual block explorer searches.

Highlights:
- Provides real-time Solana wallet balances and token portfolio tracking
- Detects large-scale whale transfers with configurable movement thresholds
- Retrieves NFT metadata, collection details, and floor price information

---

## JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server
GitHub: https://github.com/JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 2
Headless systemd deployment with Nous Portal integration — production-ready

This project provides a deployment framework for running Hermes Agent as a persistent, headless Linux service. It utilizes systemd to manage the Hermes Gateway, ensuring the agent remains active across reboots without requiring an open terminal session. The setup integrates with the Nous Portal for model access and leverages the native Hermes cron scheduler to automate recurring AI tasks. This approach offers a stable, self-hosted alternative to manual shell loops or third-party hosting for autonomous server operations.

Highlights:
- Configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service
- Enables autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs
- Supports headless operation on Ubuntu and Debian servers

---

## bryercowan/hermes-embodied

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/bryercowan/hermes-embodied
GitHub: https://github.com/bryercowan/hermes-embodied
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 2
Self-improving robotics via VLA model fine-tuning on cloud GPUs

Hermes Embodied is a robotics extension for Hermes Agent that enables autonomous fine-tuning of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models through natural language. The system orchestrates a self-improvement loop by collecting robot trajectories in simulation or hardware, provisioning cloud GPUs via Vast.ai, and retraining models when performance thresholds are met. It integrates with the LeRobot framework and MuJoCo simulation to automate the transition from data collection to model promotion. This allows users to manage complex robotics training pipelines without manual machine learning expertise.

Highlights:
- Automates VLA fine-tuning loops using natural language commands
- Provisions and manages Vast.ai cloud GPU instances autonomously
- Supports SmolVLA and GR00T models for simulation or physical hardware

---

## martymcenroe/HermesWiki

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/martymcenroe/HermesWiki
GitHub: https://github.com/martymcenroe/HermesWiki
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 2
Community wiki with deployment patterns and recipes

HermesWiki is a centralized documentation repository designed to support users of the Hermes autonomous AI email agent. It provides a structured knowledge base that outlines deployment patterns and practical implementation recipes for the agent. By consolidating technical guidance, the project helps developers configure and optimize their AI-driven email workflows. These resources ensure that users can effectively navigate the setup and operational requirements of the Hermes ecosystem.

Highlights:
- Centralized documentation for the Hermes autonomous email agent
- Provides specific deployment patterns for technical implementation
- Contains practical recipes for configuring AI email workflows

---

## raulvidis/hermes-cloudflare

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/raulvidis/hermes-cloudflare
GitHub: https://github.com/raulvidis/hermes-cloudflare
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 1
Cloudflare Browser Rendering plugin — crawl, scrape, extract content from web pages

This plugin integrates Cloudflare's Browser Rendering API into the Hermes Agent ecosystem to enable web crawling and content extraction from JavaScript-heavy websites. It utilizes Cloudflare's headless browser infrastructure to perform tasks like CSS-selector scraping, PDF generation, and full-page screenshot capture. The tool also features AI-powered structured data extraction using Llama 3.3 70B via Workers AI. Users can automate complex web workflows including sitemap discovery and Markdown conversion directly through agent commands.

Highlights:
- Crawls websites with depth controls and sitemap discovery support
- Extracts structured JSON data using Workers AI and Llama 3.3
- Converts fully rendered JavaScript-heavy pages into clean Markdown

---

## ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes
GitHub: https://github.com/ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 1
Docker Compose stack with Portainer UI and ttyd web terminal

This project provides a Docker Compose configuration for deploying the Hermes agent alongside a ttyd web terminal. It utilizes a pre-built image from the GitHub Container Registry to facilitate browser-based terminal access to the agent. Users can manage the deployment as a Git-based stack within the Portainer UI for simplified container orchestration. The setup includes an automated GitHub Action workflow for building and updating the underlying container image.

Highlights:
- Deploys Hermes agent with a ttyd web terminal interface
- Integrates directly with Portainer as a Git Repository stack
- Automates image builds using GitHub Actions and GHCR

---

## beardthelion/hermes-skill-distillation

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/beardthelion/hermes-skill-distillation
GitHub: https://github.com/beardthelion/hermes-skill-distillation
Category: Forks & Derivatives
Stars: 1
Generate agentic training trajectories from real-world tasks for Hermes 4 fine-tuning

This project provides a skill distillation pipeline designed to fine-tune Hermes 4 models using real-world agentic trajectories. It captures tool-use sessions from the Hermes agent and converts them into high-quality training data through a specialized environment and automated scoring system. The pipeline supports both offline SFT data generation and live reinforcement learning via integration with the Atropos training framework. By focusing on grounded tasks like coding and system administration, it enables a closed learning loop where agent performance directly informs model improvements.

Highlights:
- Generates SFT-ready JSONL data from 30 diverse real-world task categories
- Automates trajectory scoring based on completion, efficiency, and error recovery
- Integrates with Atropos for live reinforcement learning and model evaluation

---

## kevinmarmstrong/hermes-claude-code-rc

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/kevinmarmstrong/hermes-claude-code-rc
GitHub: https://github.com/kevinmarmstrong/hermes-claude-code-rc
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 1
Hermes Agent plugin for remote controlling Claude Code sessions via Telegram

This Hermes Agent plugin enables remote management of Claude Code sessions through a Telegram interface. It bridges the gap between mobile devices and local filesystems by allowing users to initiate authenticated remote control sessions on their host machine via slash commands. The plugin captures the resulting session URL and returns it to the user, enabling full access to local tools and databases from the Claude mobile app. Beyond session management, it supports headless command execution and direct shell access with built-in safety filters for dangerous operations.

Highlights:
- Starts Claude Code remote control sessions via Telegram slash commands
- Provides mobile access to local filesystems and MCP servers
- Executes headless commands and shell operations on the host machine
